$comment: D-CSUBSET-FOR-INIT-SCOPE (C99 6.8.5.3): sibling and nested `for (int i = ...)` loops re-declaring the same loop name compile and run — each for-init has its own scope. A ubiquitous idiom that a declare-then-assign battery never exercises. exitCode 42. The `release` arm proves the optimizer (Mem2Reg/LICM over the loop induction vars) preserves the semantics with the per-for scoping.
